content: Treatment depends on type, stage, and risk. Classical HL: early stage - ABVD (doxorubicin, bleomycin, vinblastine, dacarbazine) x2-4 cycles + involved-site radiation; advanced stage - ABVD x6 cycles or A-AVD (brentuximab vedotin replaces bleomycin, ECHELON-1 trial); relapsed - salvage chemotherapy + autologous transplant; brentuximab vedotin or checkpoint inhibitors (nivolumab, pembrolizumab) for refractory. DLBCL: R-CHOP (rituximab, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, prednisone) x6 cycles standard; Pola-R-CHP (polatuzumab) for high-risk (POLARIS); CNS prophylaxis for high-risk sites; CAR-T (axicabtagene, tisagenlecleucel, lisocabtagene) for relapsed/refractory. Follicular lymphoma: asymptomatic low-burden - observation; symptomatic/bulky - rituximab + chemotherapy (bendamustine, CHOP, CVP); rituximab maintenance; lenalidomide + rituximab alternative. Mantle cell: intensive regimens for fit young patients (cytarabine-containing) + autologous transplant; BTK inhibitors (ibrutinib, acalabrutinib, zanubrutinib) for relapsed. Marginal zone: rituximab ± chemotherapy; H. pylori eradication for gastric MALT lymphoma.
